steps.text: Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.
Establish a written process for approving conference funding which includes the elements described.
Document review and approval of conference funding by the CC/CO/NC including a copy of the proposal, how the review was conducted, criteria for review, and whether funding was approved.
Provide organization’s mission.
Provide purpose of the conference.
Provide organizer's demonstrated capacity to plan and conduct the meeting.
Provide proposed content and format.
Provide intended audience.
Provide information on other cosponsors, their mission, and their role in the conference.
Review and approve or disapprove all requests submitted to the CC/CO/NC.
Provide written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval if not sponsoring.
Provide written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval if sponsoring.
Complete co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor after final approval.
Ensure all conferences are held in ADAAG compliant facilities.

constraints.text: No verbal disapproval of sponsorship may be provided.
No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
CDC reserves the right to review all aspects of the conference.
All conferences sponsored by CDC must meet accessibility requirements under the ADAAG.

steps.text: Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG.
Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist.
Maintain documentation required as per Section 4H.
Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding conferences.
Make agreements and records concerning the amounts, sources, and uses of funds available to the public upon request.
Assign a specific person responsibility for maintaining these records.
Submit a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC.
Maintain records of the line listings and make them available to the CC/CO/NCs upon request.

constraints.text: CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.
